payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to select work
payload.justification: SG915 establishes claiming as a strategic process performed by children throughout the week.

payload.description: Task definition process for establishing compensated activities
payload.justification: SG916 identifies parental task definition as a strategically motivated process.

payload.description: Reporting process enabling pocket money distribution accuracy
payload.justification: SG919 identifies reporting as a strategic need for enabling accurate distribution.

payload.description: Historical tracking process for past activity visibility
payload.justification: SG920 establishes historical tracking as a strategic requirement, implying a process capability.

payload.description: Pocket money system comprising compensated task units claimable by children and earnings allocated to them
payload.justification: SG915 and SG916 establish tasks as core strategic entities that children claim and parents define with compensation. | SG915 establishes pocket money as the core purpose; SG918 and SG919 reinforce it as a strategic entity requiring transparency and distribution.

payload.description: Task completion status relevant to earnings calculation
payload.justification: SG917 identifies completed work visibility as strategically important, implying completion status as a key attribute.

payload.description: Weekly earnings amount for transparency reporting
payload.justification: SG918 explicitly identifies weekly earnings as requiring transparency, making the amount a strategic attribute.

payload.description: Child claims Task relationship enabling work allocation
payload.justification: SG915 describes children claiming tasks as a core strategic relationship.

payload.description: Weekly cycle governing task claiming and earnings calculation
payload.justification: SG915 mentions tasks throughout the week; SG918 specifies weekly earnings, establishing a weekly strategic cycle.

payload.description: Child actor claiming and completing tasks
payload.justification: SG915, SG917, and SG918 identify children as primary actors claiming tasks and requiring visibility and earnings transparency.

payload.description: Parent actor defining tasks and distributing pocket money
payload.justification: SG916 and SG919 identify parents as actors defining tasks and using reporting for distribution.

payload.description: Enable children to claim tasks for pocket money
payload.justification: SG915 explicitly establishes this as the core strategic purpose.

payload.description: Provide children with visibility into available and completed work, including weekly earnings transparency
payload.justification: SG917 articulates motivational intent for work visibility as a strategic goal. | SG918 explicitly articulates transparency of weekly earnings as a strategic purpose.

payload.description: Enable accurate pocket money distribution through reporting
payload.justification: SG919 identifies the parental need for reporting to enable accurate distribution as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Maintain historical tracking capability for past activities
payload.justification: SG920 expresses historical tracking as a strategic requirement, representing a goal for system capability.

payload.description: Task definition process by parent
payload.justification: Parent capability to define tasks represents a business process for creating task entities with values.

payload.description: Task claiming and tracking process
payload.justification: Core system purpose establishes claiming and tracking as fundamental business processes.

payload.description: Child task viewing process
payload.justification: Child capability to view available tasks and completed tasks represents a business process workflow.

payload.description: Earnings aggregation calculation process
payload.justification: Week-end reporting with aggregated earnings calculation represents a business process.

payload.description: Parent payment reconciliation reporting process
payload.justification: Parent reporting capability for earnings and task details represents a reconciliation business process.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset rule
payload.justification: Explicit business rule governing task availability state reset at weekly period boundaries.

payload.description: Task business entity with monetary value
payload.justification: Signals explicitly describe task entities with associated monetary value attributes as a core business data object.

payload.description: Child business entity
payload.justification: Child entity is explicitly referenced as a business entity linked through task completion relationships.

payload.description: Monetary value attribute of task
payload.justification: Monetary value is explicitly described as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Task availability state attribute
payload.justification: Task availability state is described as a data attribute that is reset at period boundaries.

payload.description: Task completion relationship between child and task
payload.justification: Signals explicitly describe conceptual relationship linking task entity and child entity through completion.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle
payload.justification: Weekly cycle is explicitly described as the temporal period governing task availability and pocket money tracking.

payload.description: Week-end reporting event
payload.justification: Week-end reporting represents a temporal business event when earnings calculations are provided.

payload.description: Weekly period boundary trigger
payload.justification: Period boundaries trigger the business rule that resets task availability state.

payload.description: Child actor
payload.justification: Children are explicitly described as business actors with capabilities to claim tasks, view information, and receive earnings reports.

payload.description: Parent actor
payload.justification: Parents are explicitly described as business actors with capabilities to define tasks and receive payment reconciliation reports.

payload.description: Enable children to earn task-based pocket money
payload.justification: Core conceptual purpose establishes the primary business goal of the system.

payload.description: Historical task completion data must persist across cycles
payload.justification: Requirement for temporal persistence and accessibility of historical data represents a business constraint.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines who participates in the household economy and their respective accountabilities—children as work claimants and performers, parents as work definers and compensation distributors.

payload.name: Work-for-Compensation Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to maintaining a bounded system of compensated work units that children may select, perform, and receive earnings for—establishing the foundational accountability for task availability and completion-based entitlement.

payload.name: Financial Transparency Governance
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to provide clear visibility into earnings accrual and work history, ensuring all participants can verify compensation accuracy and trace past economic activity within the household.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, establishing, and governing the lifecycle of value-bearing tasks within the household, including their availability, state transitions, and weekly renewal obligations.

payload.name: Participation Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing child engagement with tasks, including claiming, completion attestation, and the establishment of persistent participation records that span business cycles.

payload.name: Financial Settlement Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for computing earned value from completed obligations and reconciling payment accountability between stakeholders at defined intervals.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for child participation in the household economy through task claiming.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to complete tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for child participation in the household economy through task completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for parent participation in task definition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to distribute pocket money.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for parent participation in compensation distribution.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a pocket money system comprising compensated tasks claimable by children and earnings allocated to them.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to maintain the fundamental structural composition of the pocket money system—tasks, children, and earnings—as identified entities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status for purposes of earnings calculation.
payload.rationale: Enterprise accountability for the structural attribute of tasks that determines earnings entitlement.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish the relationship between children and tasks that enables work allocation.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to the structural relationship enabling children to claim tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to provide the capability for children to select work, satisfying both the process obligation and the motivational goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish compensated tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise accountability for defining the work units that constitute the compensated activities children may perform.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern task claiming on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to a temporal governance rule bounding when task claiming occurs.
payload.fit_criteria: Task claiming shall recur at seven-day intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern earnings calculation on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to a temporal governance rule bounding when earnings calculation occurs.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings calculation shall recur at seven-day intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to view available tasks as a foundation for transparency.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to view completed tasks as a foundation for transparency.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to transparency regarding compensation accrual; the What/Attribute CCI identifies the earnings amount as the object of visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable accurate pocket money distribution.
payload.rationale: Enterprise obligation to support distribution accuracy through reporting capability; How/Process CCI identifies the reporting mechanism supporting this goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of past tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise accountability for preserving task history to support traceability of past economic activity; How/Process CCI identifies the tracking mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance that enables the actor capability. Complementary to the actor statement, expressing what the system provides at the boundary without describing how.

payload.statement: The system shall enable task completion attestation by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing the capability for children to attest task completion, complementing the actor capability.

payload.statement: The system shall enable task definition by parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing the capability for parents to define tasks, complementing the actor capability.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing visibility into calculated earnings, supporting parent distribution decisions.

payload.statement: A task shall comprise a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion about the task entity, identifying its composition with a monetary value attribute. Refines the compensated tasks component of R005.

payload.statement: A task shall comprise an availability state.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion about the task entity's availability state, supporting the claimability aspect of R005.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's task completions.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain the participation record that establishes earnings allocation. Refines the earnings-allocation component of R005 at the business boundary.

payload.statement: A task completion shall associate a child with a task.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion defining the task completion relationship entity. Refines R006 by identifying the structural basis for completion status.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain task completion status for each child-task association.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain the completion status that supports earnings calculation. Refines R006 at the business boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall establish a claimable relationship between children and tasks.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to create the relationship mechanism. Represents the How-column process obligation for establishing the claimable association.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of available tasks.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain the task artefact that participates in the claimable relationship. Represents the What-column entity obligation.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly task availability reset cycle.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing the temporal cycle for task claiming. Refines R010 by expressing the weekly governance constraint at the business level.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability state shall reset at each weekly period boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall calculate earnings on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing the temporal cycle for earnings calculation. Refines R011 by expressing the weekly calculation constraint.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings aggregation shall occur at each weekly period boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing visibility into available tasks, complementing the actor capability.

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion history visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing visibility into completion history, complementing the actor capability.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing visibility into earnings, complementing the actor capability.

payload.statement: The business shall calculate earnings by aggregating the monetary value of each child's completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to perform earnings calculation that supports accurate distribution. Refines R015 by identifying the business-level calculation responsibility.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completed task.
payload.rationale: Row 1 seed requires historical records of past tasks; at Row 2 this manifests as the business responsibility to maintain a persistent record of each task completion relationship, supporting the constraint that historical data must persist across cycles.

payload.statement: Historical task completion data shall persist across weekly business cycles.
payload.rationale: The seed's historical record obligation imposes a persistence constraint at Row 2: completed task data must survive the weekly reset cycle that renews task availability.
payload.fit_criteria: Task completion records remain queryable after the weekly period boundary has passed.

payload.statement: The system shall make historical task completion data available to authorised actors.
payload.rationale: To realize the enterprise's historical record commitment, the system must provide a black-box capability that presents past task data to children and parents, enabling the viewing processes referenced in the source domain.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a Task entity bearing monetary value.
payload.rationale: Task is the central business artefact with a structural monetary value attribute; both composition and attribute are asserted in the source CCIs.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a task availability state for each task.
payload.rationale: Task availability state is a structural attribute of the Task entity that the business must maintain to govern lifecycle transitions.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce weekly task availability reset.
payload.rationale: How-column rule combined with When-column cycle and trigger; this is a business governance constraint tied to the weekly boundary that resets task states.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing the capability for task claiming at the boundary, complementary to the actor-action capability.

payload.statement: The system shall make assigned tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing the capability for task viewing at the boundary, complementary to the actor-action capability.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a child as a participant entity.
payload.rationale: Business recognition of the child entity required to support participation accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task completion by a child.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to record task completion relationships as evidence of child participation, supporting the claiming and tracking process.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Business capability declaration derived from the goal to enable child earning through task-based participation.

payload.statement: The business shall preserve task completion records across weekly cycles.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint requiring historical persistence of participation data beyond individual business cycles.

payload.statement: A task shall carry a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural obligation derived from the What-column attribute CCI: each task entity must possess a monetary value attribute to enable earnings computation.

payload.statement: The business shall compute aggregated earnings from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for the earnings aggregation calculation process, expressing what the business does behind the boundary to compute earned value.

payload.statement: The business shall produce a payment reconciliation report for each parent.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for reconciling payment accountability to parent stakeholders, combining the reconciliation process and the parent actor for whom the report is produced.

payload.statement: The business shall execute settlement reporting at the end of each week.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to enforce the weekly settlement cycle, integrating the week-end reporting event and the weekly period boundary trigger into a single temporal obligation.

payload.statement: A child can claim an available task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing capability to claim tasks, enabled by the system. The child is the external party reaching in; claiming is the real action.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance statement expressing WHAT the system provides at the boundary (the capability enabling child claiming), without specifying HOW it is realised.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task claim.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to maintain claim records, supporting participation accountability and historical persistence.

payload.statement: A parent can define a task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's boundary-crossing capability to define tasks. The parent is the external party; task definition is their real action.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition available to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance expressing WHAT the system provides (task definition capability) without specifying the technical realisation mechanism.

payload.statement: A task shall consist of a monetary value and an availability state.
payload.rationale: Structural requirement surfaced through object-recursion interrogative applied to the task entity. The task's composition (monetary value and availability state attributes) is a structural assertion necessary to establish compensated tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly task availability reset.
payload.rationale: Constraint requirement expressing the WHY/HOW-column business governance rule that tasks must reset weekly. This is a business-level rule enforcing the weekly cycle trigger, not a system-level operation.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability states are reset at each weekly period boundary
